draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prioritize scannable client overviews (cards with photo, status, next action) over dense tabular contact records
facade.axisTargeted: Information density: dense tables vs. scannable cards
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: Show client data in visual card format; prioritize visual hierarchy and quick pattern recognition over exhaustive data rows
facade.rejectImplies: Spreadsheet-style tables with many columns are acceptable; users prefer comprehensive data visibility over visual simplicity

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prioritize scannable client overviews (cards with photo, status, next action) over dense tabular contact records
evidence.implication: Show client data in visual card format; prioritize visual hierarchy and quick pattern recognition over exhaustive data rows

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer high contrast layouts that separate navigation from workspace
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light content
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark UI patterns for the CRM — navigation dark, canvas light. Test if this reduces cognitive load when managing multiple clients.
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward unified light or dark mode. Dark sidebars may feel cramped for a tool meant to feel open and collaborative.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer direct, no-nonsense language over corporate jargon in their CRM interface
facade.label: Straight-talking tone
facade.acceptImplies: Adopt clear, conversational copy; use 'you' and imperative verbs; strip buzzwords
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into industry terminology and formal phrasing; assume users want 'enterprise-grade' language

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer notifications that appear without animation—fast, no-nonsense alerts for incoming leads and messages
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and transition style — animated entrance vs instant appearance
facade.label: Instant popups
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and directness in alerts; likely prefers snappy, minimal-motion interactions throughout the CRM
facade.rejectImplies: User expects motion to soften interactions; animated popups, slides, or fades may feel more polished and less jarring to this builder

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want visual depth and hierarchy in their client overview—not flat design.
facade.label: Dashboard cards with shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward warm, layered interfaces with depth cues and subtle elevation. Consider card-based layouts, soft shadows, and tactile micro-interactions.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward cool, flat surfaces with strong typography and minimal spatial tricks. Consider grid-based layouts, high contrast, and stark geometric boundaries.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent left sidebar for navigation paired with card-based layouts for client/project information, balancing quick access to core functions with scannable content chunks.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure × content layout (sidebar vs tabs) AND (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward columnar layouts with stable left navigation and modular card displays—likely values quick context-switching between clients and projects.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tabs, full-width layouts, or dense lists—suggests preference for either tabbed mental models or information density over spatial separation.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor a dashboard-first overview vs. a task-list-first workflow—reveals how they mentally organize client work and priorities
Whether they want a professional, formal tone vs. a casual, encouraging tone in copy and feedback—reveals comfort with personality in business tools
Whether they prefer a calm, zen workspace vs. an energizing, motivational one—reveals emotional context they need while managing multiple clients
Whether they want all client info on one screen vs. organized into separate focused views—reveals tolerance for information density and navigation depth
Whether they prefer instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals if they value speed/directness or polish/delight in interaction

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor a dashboard-first overview vs. a task-list-first workflow—reveals how they mentally organize client work and priorities
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want a professional, formal tone vs. a casual, encouraging tone in copy and feedback—reveals comfort with personality in business tools
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer a calm, zen workspace vs. an energizing, motivational one—reveals emotional context they need while managing multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want all client info on one screen vs. organized into separate focused views—reveals tolerance for information density and navigation depth
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals if they value speed/directness or polish/delight in interaction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need a chronological project progression view (deadlines, milestones, delivery dates) rather than static client snapshots to manage work-in-progress and upcoming deliverables
facade.axisTargeted: dense data (timeline/project states) vs key metrics (current status only)
facade.label: Timeline view
facade.acceptImplies: Design should emphasize temporal navigation and project phase visibility; include calendar, Gantt, or milestone progression views as primary interface
facade.rejectImplies: Temporal data is secondary; prioritize relationship and contact management over project timeline visualization; focus on client state snapshots instead of work progression
